originalBehavior: Chapter-based slideshow progression with scratch transitions, sound controls, slide selection, and the original pacing across intro, one-off, iterated, tournament, distrust, noise, sandbox, conclusion, credits, and footnotes.
localStatus: Vendored from upstream and kept structurally intact; metadata, share URL handling, footer-only reference links, and public comment cleanup were applied around the runtime.
notes: Narrative order still comes from upstream slide modules rather than a rewritten wrapper.
Translation-guide and original-English comment blocks were removed from the public HTML while docs retain provenance.
Auxiliary footnotes and credits remain in the vendored trust subtree.
evidence: Live page walkthrough cross-checked at https://ncase.me/trust/ on 2026-03-10.

originalBehavior: One-off prisoner's dilemma payoff combinations update scores, labels, and visible outcomes while the user explores cooperate versus defect choices.
localStatus: Preserved from upstream with the original payoff UI assets and one-off simulation code.
notes: Payoff interactions still live inside the original slide-specific orchestration.
No local refactor was introduced before parity smoke tests.
evidence: Behavior called out in the local audit inventory as the payoff board module.

originalBehavior: Strategy controls switch between copycat, always cheat, always cooperate, random, and other predefined behaviors that immediately change round outcomes and narration.
localStatus: Strategy selection is still driven by upstream slide logic and button controls.
notes: Button timing, text reveals, and score changes are upstream behavior.
The local page does not wrap or replace the strategy state machine.
evidence: Local audit module inventory lists strategy selection as a core interaction.
Live page behavior confirmed visually during implementation review.

originalBehavior: Repeated-round sequences show retaliation, forgiveness, and exploitation over multiple turns with scoreboards, animations, and sound cues.
localStatus: Iterated play remains mapped to the original simulation and slide choreography.
notes: Round progression and replay are controlled by the vendored iterated module.
Supporting art and scoreboard assets stay in their upstream relative locations.
evidence: Local audit notes describe the repeated-round simulator and history updates.
Source tree contains dedicated iterated assets and slide module wiring.

originalBehavior: Population tournament and evolution sections pit mixed strategies against each other, update standings over many interactions, and transition into the broader trust narrative.
localStatus: Tournament and evolution scenes are imported directly from the upstream slideshow stack.
notes: Population visuals, ranking updates, and chapter transitions remain upstream-driven.
No SPA router or page shell intercepts the tournament runtime.
evidence: Tournament and evolution modules are explicitly separated in the upstream source tree.
Audit notes list population tournament behavior and dominance changes.

originalBehavior: Noise or miscommunication sections inject mistakes into repeated games so cooperation stability rises or collapses depending on the error rate.
localStatus: Noise behavior is preserved via the vendored trust slides and shared simulation logic.
notes: The local port keeps the original slider-driven explanation rather than rewriting it into separate widgets.
Path fixes were completed before any copy or styling edits.
evidence: Local audit notes identify noise and miscommunication controls as a dedicated module.
Live page comparison on 2026-03-10 showed the same narrative placement.

originalBehavior: The later sandbox lets users vary forgiveness, punishment, and related policy controls to test which interventions can rebuild trust under noisy conditions.
localStatus: Sandbox UI and policy controls stay in the original upstream files, with only the surrounding replica note added.
notes: Increment/decrement controls and tabs are still provided by the upstream UI art and scripts.
This section was kept late in the porting sequence so interaction parity could be checked first.
Reachable public subroutes such as notes and peeps now use the same single-link footer pattern.
evidence: Local audit notes call out policy sandbox behavior and long-run equilibrium changes.
Upstream source separates sandbox slide scripting from shared numeric controls.
